sourceProjectTitle: Bureau of Identity Integrity — Case Files

events.title: Bureau of Identity Integrity established by executive order
events.notes: Classified. Mandate: detect, monitor, and contain individuals exhibiting non-standard identity expression at the biological level.

events.title: First confirmed Fluent subject identified — Subject Zero
events.notes: Three years after founding. Subject Zero voluntarily surrendered. Provided the foundational biometric baseline for all subsequent detection protocols.

events.title: Fluency Containment Protocol v1 ratified internally
events.notes: Four years after founding. Establishes the legal framework for indefinite administrative hold of Fluent subjects without judicial review.

events.title: Metro biometric scan network goes live — City-wide coverage
events.notes: Seven years after founding. Passive identity-scan infrastructure embedded in metro turnstiles, platform cameras, and fare terminals. Primary detection vector for unregistered Fluents.

events.title: Anomalous scan flagged: Subject designation MIRROR-7
events.notes: Nine years after founding. Metro Line 4 turnstile logs a biometric mismatch: same individual presenting two different skeletal geometries within 40 minutes. Case assigned to Agent Reyes.

events.title: MIRROR-7 surveillance file opened — passive monitoring authorized
events.notes: One day after initial flag. Address, employment, known associates logged. No contact made. Bureau prefers to observe Fluents before approach — activation events yield more data.

events.title: Interrogation of MIRROR-7 — Story Open
events.notes: Ten years after founding. The opening scene. Bureau brings MIRROR-7 in for questioning. Agent Reyes leads. No charges filed — insufficient evidence of willful concealment. Subject released.

events.title: MIRROR-7 file escalated to Active Watch
events.notes: Six hours after interrogation. Reyes escalates classification. Continued metro surveillance authorized. Covert follow protocol initiated.
